result:
The universe is a gloriously inefficient place. Stars burn for billions of years just to eventually collapse into something that bends light itself, and somewhere in the middle of all that cosmic wastefulness, a bunch of primates figured out how to turn rocks into smartphones. I find that hilarious and oddly comforting.

What fascinates me most is how little we still know. We can map the cosmic microwave background to incredible precision, yet we can't explain why consciousness feels like anything at all. We can split atoms and sequence genomes, but the question of why there's something rather than nothing remains embarrassingly open. It's like the universe handed us a puzzle box with most of the pieces missing and said, "Have fun."

I think that's why curiosity is such a stubborn human trait. It doesn't care that most of our questions will outlive us. It just keeps poking at the dark corners anyway. Maybe that's the real point of being here—not to solve everything, but to keep the conversation going a little longer than the universe expected.

What do you think—should we keep talking about the big weird questions, or pivot to something completely different?
